Class SkillManager

java.lang.Object
sims.actions.SkillManager
All Implemented Interfaces:
ProgressionInterface
public class SkillManager extends Object implements ProgressionInterface
Manages skill progression for a Sim by tracking levels and experience points (XP).

The SkillManager class implements ProgressionInterface to provide a consistent progression system across the game. Each skill starts at level 1 and gains XP through actions. Once enough XP is accumulated, the skill levels up.

Core Features:

  • Tracks the current skill level.
  • Accumulates exp (experience points) through earnXP().
  • Levels up when XP reaches or exceeds 100, resetting XP while incrementing the level.

Progression Rules:

  • Initial level is set to 1.
  • Each call to earnXP() adds 25 XP.
  • When XP ≥ 100, the skill levels up and XP is reduced modulo 100.

Usage Example:

SkillManager cookingSkill = new SkillManager();
System.out.println(cookingSkill.getLevel()); // 1

cookingSkill.earnXP(); // +25 XP
cookingSkill.earnXP(); // +25 XP (total 50 XP)

// After 4 calls to earnXP(), XP = 100 → level increases to 2

This class provides a simple yet extensible framework for skill progression, allowing different skills (e.g., Hunger, Social, Fun) to share a consistent leveling system while remaining flexible for future enhancements.

    Constructs a new SkillManager with default values.

  • Constructor Details

    • SkillManager

      public SkillManager()
      Constructs a new SkillManager with default values.

      This constructor initializes the skill progression system by setting the starting level to 1. The exp (experience points) field is implicitly initialized to 0, meaning the skill begins at the base level with no XP earned.

      Usage Example:

      SkillManager cookingSkill = new SkillManager();
System.out.println(cookingSkill.getLevel()); // Outputs: 1

      By default, all skills begin at level 1 and progress upward as XP is accumulated through earnXP().

  • Method Details

    • getLevel

      public int getLevel()
      Retrieves the current skill level.

      This method returns the value of the level field, representing how advanced the skill is. Levels start at 1 and increase as experience points (XP) are earned through earnXP().

      Usage Example:

      SkillManager cookingSkill = new SkillManager();
int currentLevel = cookingSkill.getLevel(); // Returns 1 initially
      Specified by:
      getLevel in interface ProgressionInterface
      Returns:
      the current progression level of the skill

    • earnXP

      public void earnXP()
      Awards experience points (XP) to the skill and handles level progression.

      Each call to this method increases the exp field by 25 points. When exp reaches or exceeds 100, the skill levels up:

      • level is incremented by 1.
      • exp is reduced modulo 100, carrying over any remainder.

      Progression Rules:

      • Initial XP starts at 0.
      • Each call adds 25 XP.
      • Level increases once XP ≥ 100.
      • XP resets to the remainder after leveling up.

      Usage Example:

      SkillManager cookingSkill = new SkillManager();
cookingSkill.earnXP(); // exp = 25, level = 1
cookingSkill.earnXP(); // exp = 50, level = 1
cookingSkill.earnXP(); // exp = 75, level = 1
cookingSkill.earnXP(); // exp = 100 → level = 2, exp = 0

      This method provides a simple progression mechanic where skills advance steadily as XP is earned through repeated actions.

      Specified by:
      earnXP in interface ProgressionInterface
